summary: Decompilation progress for the Shrine Route ground stage file, primarily adding C bodies and partial matches in src/melee/gr/grshrineroute.c, with supporting type/header updates in gr/types.h and gr/grshrineroute.h. The bot report records 6 new matches and 7 improved unmatched items for main/melee/gr/grshrineroute.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; adds includes, data extern/type declarations, and implementations or partial implementations for Shrine Route stage functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: includes added for camera, gm_1601, granime, grlib, stage, lb_00F9, ftdevice, lbvector, math_ppc, trigf
raw_diff: added bodies for fn_80208A38, grShrineRoute_80208D14, grShrineRoute_80208F70 excerpted
text_corpus: bot report lists main/melee/gr/grshrineroute new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Supporting ground type updates for Shrine Route state layout.

what_changed.text: Replaced decomp placeholders for at least fn_80208A38 and grShrineRoute_80208D14 with C implementations in grshrineroute.c.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: -/// #fn_80208A38 replaced by void fn_80208A38(HSD_GObj* gobj)
raw_diff: -/// #grShrineRoute_80208D14 replaced by void grShrineRoute_80208D14(Ground_GObj* gobj)

what_changed.text: Added a large C implementation for grShrineRoute_80208F70 that drives Shrine Route state transitions, material changes, camera position updates, platform tracking, and player/stage interactions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: switch on gp->gv.shrineroute.xC4 with cases 0 through 6
raw_diff: calls to Camera_80030AE0, Ground_801C38BC, Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset, grMaterial_801C9604, mpJointListAdd, mpLib_80057BC0

what_changed.text: Added include dependencies needed by the new Shrine Route code, including camera, game, ground animation/lib/stage, fighter, vector, and MSL math/trig headers.

Adjusted local data declarations in grshrineroute.c, including changing two fields in the grSh_Route_804D6A58 pointed-to struct from int to f32 and adding extern declarations for light descriptors, Vec3 constants, and grSh_Route_LightConfig.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: x1C and x20 changed from int to f32

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded 6 new matches and 7 improvements in main/melee/gr/grshrineroute, with overall matched code +1980 bytes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report says Matched code 62.06% (+0.05%, +1980 bytes)
text_corpus: 7 improvements in unmatched items table

smart_moves.text: The PR used typed Ground/GObj/JObj access and the shrineroute GroundVars arm rather than raw pointer arithmetic in the shown implementations.
Repeated Shrine Route setup and platform/symbol initialization was expressed with small for-loops where indices vary naturally.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) initializes symbols to NULL
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) initializes platforms from Ground_801C2CF4(i + 0x7F)
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) in fn_80208A38 creates symbol effects

smart_moves.text: A state machine in grShrineRoute_80208F70 was represented as a switch on gp->gv.shrineroute.xC4 rather than a generated branch ladder in the shown excerpt.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stage decompilation, recovered GroundVars fields can make large state-machine functions reviewable by replacing anonymous storage with a subsystem-specific union arm.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated gp->gv.shrineroute field access in added functions

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a ground stage function performs repeated joint or platform setup over contiguous IDs, natural loops are appropriate when the PR evidence shows only the index or ID offset changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) with Ground_801C2CF4(i + 0xBD)
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) with Ground_801C2CF4(i + 0x7F)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large stage update functions may combine gameplay state, camera smoothing, material transitions, and collision joint toggles; separating the state machine with switch cases and scoped locals improves readability while retaining address-style symbol names when semantics are not proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: camera calls Camera_80030AE0 and Ground_801C38BC
raw_diff: collision/joint calls mpLib_80057BC0 and mpJointListAdd
raw_diff: address-style function names such as grShrineRoute_8020B020 retained

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for unresolved Shrine Route functions and data, e.g. grShrineRoute_80208F70, fn_80208A38, grSh_Route_804D6A58, and grSh_Route_803E5A58.
Short role names were used for common local object pointers and vectors, including gp for Ground*, gobj for Ground_GObj/HSD_GObj, jobj/ejobj for HSD_JObj*, and player for HSD_GObj* player.
Stack-vector temporaries in grShrineRoute_80208F70 retain stack-offset-style names such as sp88, sp7C, sp5C, sp50, and sp44, indicating semantics were not fully renamed in the shown excerpt.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Vec3 sp88; Vec3 sp7C; Vec3 sp68; Vec3 sp5C; Vec3 sp50; Vec3 sp44

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK in newly implemented functions to preserve stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice contains bot match/improvement evidence but no inline author explanation for why each PAD_STACK was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used HSD_ASSERT macro rather than a raw assert block for the symbol existence check in grShrineRoute_80208F70.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No review discussion confirms whether this was derived from assert macro recovery or already present in decompiler output.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used GET_JOBJ and HSD_JObjSet*/Get* helpers for object transform work.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The loaded evidence does not include review or objdiff notes specifically validating GET_JOBJ usage.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expressed repeated initialization and platform tracking with for-loops rather than repeated copied blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) symbol effect setup
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) platform setup
raw_diff: for (k = 0; k < 3; k++) platform translate update
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The diff excerpt is truncated after the platform update loop begins, so only the visible portion is evidenced.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments are present in the loaded PR slice.
Automated bot feedback reported measurable decomp progress: 6 new matches, 7 improved unmatched items, and +1980 matched bytes.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/gr/grshrineroute.c and grShrineRoute_80208F70 for cleanup or full match completion.
Search for PAD_STACK usage in grshrineroute.c to see whether later cleanup removed stack padding.
Search for gp->gv.shrineroute field names in src/melee/gr/types.h to determine whether xC4/xC6/xC8/xCE/xD4 were later semantically renamed.
Search for GET_JOBJ usage in ground stage code near grshrineroute to compare accepted local style.
Search for grShrineRoute_8020A8A4, grShrineRoute_8020AA40, grShrineRoute_8020AB58, and grShrineRoute_8020AE08 in the full diff or later source to capture tactics not visible in the truncated excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed GroundVars arms to make stage state-machine decomp readable
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2384 shows Shrine Route stage code using gp->gv.shrineroute fields throughout newly added C bodies, with a paired gr/types.h update. This supports a local lesson that stage decomp should recover and use the relevant GroundVars arm when it can replace anonymous storage in large update functions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer natural loops for contiguous stage joint/platform setup when only IDs or offsets vary
accepted_candidate_records.text: The visible Shrine Route implementations use for-loops for six symbol effects and three platform entries where each iteration changes only an index or joint ID offset, matching the natural-loops standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for-loop over i < 6 with i + 0xBD
raw_diff: for-loop over i < 3 with i + 0x7F

source_update_candidates.title: grshrineroute.c is a stage/ground decompilation file for Shrine Route
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2384 changed src/melee/gr/grshrineroute.c heavily and the bot report identifies the unit as main/melee/gr/grshrineroute with multiple Shrine Route function matches and improvements.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path classification for future PR intake and curator searches.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK evidence reminders for stage decomp
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2384 uses PAD_STACK in multiple newly added Shrine Route functions while only automated match-progress evidence is loaded. Consider whether standards should remind curators to capture narrow objdiff/build evidence for retained PAD_STACK in stage code.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists matches/improvements but no tactic-specific rationale
source_update_candidates.reason: This is already broadly covered by matching-tactics-need-evidence, but this PR is a concrete example candidate; curator should decide whether to add no new standard or a narrower note.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every shown function fully matched; the bot report lists grShrineRoute_80208F70 and fn_80208A38 as improved but still below 100%.
Do not treat PAD_STACK usage as accepted style without the missing objdiff/build rationale.
Do not infer human reviewer intent because the loaded slice has no human review comments.
Do not promote semantic names for xC4/xC6/xC8/xCE/xD4 or grSh_Route_* data; the PR evidence keeps address/offset-style names.
